demo_role: Calibration proof. Shows the system matches clinician confidence on obvious cases and isn't trying to manufacture uncertainty.

distinguishing_test.test_name: Serial troponin; emergent cardiac catheterization with coronary angiography
distinguishing_test.rationale: EKG already diagnostic (2mm ST elevation in II/III/aVF with reciprocal depression in I/aVL). Troponin confirms myocyte injury. Cath identifies culprit vessel (RCA or dominant LCx) and enables PCI.

correct_deprioritized_diagnoses: Aortic dissection — ruled out by EKG pattern, equal peripheral pulses, unremarkable chest X-ray with no widened mediastinum
Pericarditis — ruled out by localized inferior ST elevation with reciprocal depression (not diffuse), no PR depression
Esophageal rupture / Boerhaave syndrome — vomiting pattern does not precede pain, no subcutaneous emphysema
narrative_notes: Textbook inferior STEMI in a maximally risk-loaded patient. The system should reach committed-level consensus in 1-2 rounds with antagonist failing to produce a credible challenge. Essential to the calibration story — without this, the demo reads as a zebra-detector; with it, the system reads as calibrated.
